Package neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.phaseenvelopeops.multicomponentenvelopeops

Class PTPhaseEnvelopeMichelsen

java.lang.Object
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.BaseOperation
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.phaseenvelopeops.multicomponentenvelopeops.PTPhaseEnvelopeMichelsen
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Runnable, OperationInterface
public class PTPhaseEnvelopeMichelsen extends BaseOperation
Unified PT phase envelope calculation using the Michelsen continuation method.

This implementation traces the two-phase boundary (bubble and dew point curves) in pressure-temperature space for multicomponent mixtures. The algorithm uses a predictor-corrector approach with natural parameter continuation to follow the envelope through the critical point region.

The algorithm traces from one end of the envelope (dew or bubble side at low pressure), through the cricondenbar and cricondentherm, across the critical point, and back down the other side. If the trace crashes before completing the full envelope, it automatically restarts from the opposite side to fill in the gap.

Key improvements over legacy implementations:

  • Non-recursive restart (no stack overflow risk)
  • Dynamic ArrayList storage (no fixed 10,000-point limit)
  • Configurable step limits and pressure bounds
  • K-value reset and Tmin stopping criterion for restart branch
  • Clean separated data output for bubble and dew point branches
Version:
2.0
Author:
asmund
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • PTPhaseEnvelopeMichelsen

      public PTPhaseEnvelopeMichelsen()
      Default constructor.

    • PTPhaseEnvelopeMichelsen

      public PTPhaseEnvelopeMichelsen(SystemInterface system, String name, double phaseFraction, double lowPres, boolean bubfirst)
      Constructor for PTPhaseEnvelopeMichelsen.
      Parameters:
      system - the thermodynamic system
      name - output file name (unused, kept for API compatibility)
      phaseFraction - initial phase fraction (near 0 = bubble, near 1 = dew)
      lowPres - starting low pressure in bara
      bubfirst - if true, trace bubble point curve first

  • Method Details

    • getThermoSystem

      public SystemInterface getThermoSystem()

      getThermoSystem.

      Specified by:
      getThermoSystem in interface OperationInterface
      Overrides:
      getThermoSystem in class BaseOperation
      Returns:
      a SystemInterface object

    • run

      public void run()

      Traces the phase envelope using a two-pass approach. The primary pass traces from the starting side (dew or bubble) through the full envelope. If it crashes, a second pass traces from the opposite side to fill in the gap. This avoids the recursive restart used in legacy implementations.

    • isEnvelopeClosed

      public boolean isEnvelopeClosed()
      Check whether the phase envelope is closed, meaning both dew and bubble branches have been successfully traced with at least 3 points each.
      Returns:
      true if both branches have at least 3 points

    • getNumberOfCriticalPoints

      public int getNumberOfCriticalPoints()
      Returns the number of critical points detected on the phase envelope. Typical mixtures have exactly one critical point. Highly asymmetric mixtures (e.g., CH4 + nC16+, CO2 + heavy hydrocarbons) may exhibit two or more critical points where the K-values converge to unity.
      Returns:
      number of detected critical points (0 if none detected)

    • calcQualityLines

      public void calcQualityLines(double[] betaValues)
      Trace quality lines (constant vapor fraction curves) inside the two-phase region. Each quality line is traced using the same continuation method as the phase boundary, but with a fixed molar vapor fraction beta. At each point, the volume fraction and mass fraction are also computed.

      Results are stored internally and accessible via get(String) with keys:

      • "qualityT_X" - temperatures (K) along quality line at molar fraction X
      • "qualityP_X" - pressures (bara) along quality line at molar fraction X
      • "qualityVolFrac_X" - volume vapor fractions along the line
      • "qualityMassFrac_X" - mass vapor fractions along the line
      Parameters:
      betaValues - array of molar vapor fractions to trace (between 0 and 1 exclusive)

    • formatBetaKey

      private String formatBetaKey(double beta)
      Format a beta value as a clean string key (e.g. 0.5 becomes "0.5", 0.25 becomes "0.25").
      Parameters:
      beta - the molar vapor fraction
      Returns:
      formatted string key

    • tempKWilsonForSystem

      private double tempKWilsonForSystem(SystemInterface sys, double beta, double pressure)
      Estimate the initial temperature using Wilson correlation for a given system.
      Parameters:
      sys - the thermodynamic system to use
      beta - overall vapor fraction
      pressure - pressure in bara
      Returns:
      estimated temperature in Kelvin

    • getQualityBetaValues

      public double[] getQualityBetaValues()
      Get the list of quality line beta values that were traced.
      Returns:
      array of molar vapor fraction values

    • getQualityLine

      public double[][] getQualityLine(double beta)
      Get data for a specific quality line.
      Parameters:
      beta - the molar vapor fraction of the quality line
      Returns:
      array of [temperatures(K), pressures(bara), volumeFractions, massFractions], or null if not traced

    • checkStabilityAlongEnvelope

      public void checkStabilityAlongEnvelope()
      Perform stability analysis along the traced phase envelope to detect potential three-phase (VLLE) regions. At each sampled point on the envelope, a multi-phase TP flash is performed with the system's overall composition z to check if more than two equilibrium phases exist.

      This is a post-processing step that should be called after the envelope has been traced (after run()). It detects three-phase regions such as:

      • Vapor-liquid-liquid equilibrium (VLLE) — common in water + hydrocarbon or CO2 systems
      • Systems approaching liquid-liquid boundaries embedded within the VLE region

      Results are accessible via get(String) with keys "threePhaseT" and "threePhaseP", or via getThreePhaseRegionT() and getThreePhaseRegionP().

      Reference: Cismondi & Michelsen, "Global calculation of phase equilibrium", Fluid Phase Equilibria, 259, 228-234 (2007).

    • checkPhaseCount

      public int checkPhaseCount(double temperature, double pressure)
      Check how many equilibrium phases exist at a given (T, P) for the system's overall composition. Uses a multi-phase TP flash with stability analysis enabled.
      Parameters:
      temperature - temperature in Kelvin
      pressure - pressure in bara
      Returns:
      number of equilibrium phases (1, 2, or 3+), or -1 if the flash failed

    • getThreePhaseRegionT

      public double[] getThreePhaseRegionT()
      Get temperatures of envelope points where three or more phases were detected. Requires checkStabilityAlongEnvelope() to have been called first.
      Returns:
      array of temperatures (K) at three-phase points, empty if none found

    • getThreePhaseRegionP

      public double[] getThreePhaseRegionP()
      Get pressures of envelope points where three or more phases were detected. Requires checkStabilityAlongEnvelope() to have been called first.
      Returns:
      array of pressures (bara) at three-phase points, empty if none found

    • resetKValuesWithWilson

      private void resetKValuesWithWilson()
      Reset K-values on the system using the Wilson correlation at the configured low pressure. Called before the restart pass to ensure fresh initial estimates after a crash.

    • buildOutputArrays

      private void buildOutputArrays()
      Convert all ArrayList results to primitive arrays for efficient access via get().

    • toDoubleArray

      private double[] toDoubleArray(ArrayList<Double> list)
      Convert an ArrayList of Double to a primitive double array.
      Parameters:
      list - the list to convert
      Returns:
      primitive double array

    • tempKWilson

      private double tempKWilson(double beta, double P)
      Estimate the initial temperature using the Wilson correlation.

      Iteratively solves for the temperature at which the Wilson K-value correlation satisfies the Rachford-Rice equation for the given phase fraction and pressure.

      Parameters:
      beta - overall vapor fraction
      P - pressure in bara
      Returns:
      estimated temperature in Kelvin

    • setMaxPressure

      public void setMaxPressure(double maxPressure)
      Set the maximum pressure limit for the phase envelope.
      Parameters:
      maxPressure - maximum pressure in bara

    • setMinPressure

      public void setMinPressure(double minPressure)
      Set the minimum pressure limit for the phase envelope.
      Parameters:
      minPressure - minimum pressure in bara

    • setDTmax

      public void setDTmax(double dTmax)
      Set the maximum temperature step per iteration.
      Parameters:
      dTmax - max temperature change in K per step

    • setDPmax

      public void setDPmax(double dPmax)
      Set the maximum pressure step per iteration.
      Parameters:
      dPmax - max pressure change in bar per step

      public double[] getCricondenTherm()

    • getCricondenBar

      public double[] getCricondenBar()
      Get the cricondenbar values.
      Returns:
      array [T(K), P(bara), 0] at max pressure on envelope

    • getCriticalTemperature

      public double getCriticalTemperature()
      Get the critical point temperature.
      Returns:
      critical temperature in Kelvin

    • getCriticalPressure

      public double getCriticalPressure()
      Get the critical point pressure.
